title: lopholaena brickellioides
intro: Lopholaena brickellioides, also known as the "Bushman's Poison," is a plant species in the family Asteraceae. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Lopholaena brickellioides include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory infections. Lopholaena brickellioides is often prepared as a tea or infused in water to be consumed, and it can also be used topically to apply its medicinal properties directly to the affected area.
